Coral Springs Home Loans

FHA loans in Coral Springs, Florida

Coral Springs was built as a master-planned city, and it shows: tidy neighborhoods, well-regarded schools, and a strong mix of single-family homes and townhomes that draw families. Because Broward County is a designated high-cost area, FHA buyers here have extra borrowing room — helpful in a city where families compete for move-in-ready homes in communities like Eagle Trace and Heron Bay.

Broward County FHA limit (1-unit)$667,000

2026 single-family

Minimum down3.5%

With a 580+ score

Min credit score580

Property types1–4 units

Owner-occupied

What the FHA limit means in Coral Springs

Broward County is a designated high-cost area, so its 2026 single-family FHA limit is $667,000, above the $541,287 statewide floor — meaning you can finance higher-priced Coral Springs homes than in most of Florida. Confirm Broward County's exact limit with HUD's official FHA loan-limit lookup. That higher limit helps Coral Springs families compete for single-family homes and townhomes, with only the largest luxury homes exceeding it.

FHA for families in master-planned Coral Springs

Coral Springs is a classic family market, and FHA's 3.5% down plus gift-fund allowance eases the path to ownership. Single-family homes are straightforward FHA purchases; for townhomes and condos in the planned communities, confirm the association/building is FHA-approved (or qualifies for Single-Unit Approval) — see our condo guide. Sellers can contribute up to 6% toward your closing costs.

Down payment help for Coral Springs buyers

You don't have to cover the down payment alone. FHA allows gift funds, sellers can contribute up to 6% toward closing costs, and Coral Springs buyers can often pair FHA with assistance from the Florida Housing Finance Corporation and Broward County's local SHIP-funded programs. Funding and eligibility change frequently, so we'll help you confirm what's currently available — see our FHA down payment assistance guide.

Neighborhoods we serve in Coral Springs

We help buyers throughout Coral Springs and Broward County, including Eagle Trace, Heron Bay, Maplewood, and the surrounding planned communities. Wherever you're looking, we'll explain your FHA loan limit, rate, and monthly payment — and get you pre-approved fast.

Questions & Answers

Frequently asked questions

What is the FHA loan limit in Coral Springs (Broward County) for 2026?+
Broward County is a high-cost area, so its 2026 FHA limit is $667,000, above the $541,287 Florida floor. Confirm the exact figure with HUD's official lookup.
Is Coral Springs good for FHA buyers with families?+
Yes — its master-planned communities, schools, and mix of homes and townhomes, plus Broward's higher FHA limit, make it a strong family FHA market.
Can I buy a Coral Springs townhome with FHA?+
Yes, if the association/building is FHA-approved or the unit qualifies for Single-Unit Approval. We verify before you offer.
How much down payment do I need in Coral Springs?+
As little as 3.5% with a 580+ score; gift funds and seller credits up to 6% can help.
Do you serve all of Broward County?+
Yes — Coral Springs, Parkland, Coconut Creek, Fort Lauderdale, and surrounding communities.
